title: Kreodanthus loxoglottis
intro: Kreodanthus loxoglottis, also known as the "Venus Slipper Orchid," is a rare and endangered epiphytic orchid species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases and conditions. The bioactive constituents of Kreodanthus loxoglottis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.

The genus Kreodanthus is a small group of orchids endemic to Central and South America. Kreodanthus loxoglottis is a species of orchid within this genus and is native to Costa Rica.
